title: Prepare a live metrics view for the spike

blocks.content:
A postmortem view hides how fast load ramps feel in real time. Before you click **Run** on the spike test, stage a layout you can watch without hunting menus while virtual users climb.

You can keep everything in **Explore** with two queries (one `grafanacloud-k6`, one Prometheus) as in the previous milestone, or paste panels into a temporary dashboard using **Copy to Clipboard** from Grafana Cloud k6 as described in [Correlate results in Grafana](/docs/grafana-cloud/testing/k6/analyze-results/correlate-results-in-grafana/).

To prepare the live view, complete the following steps.

1. Open the **Explore** layout or dashboard you will keep visible during the spike. Ensure one panel or query shows virtual users (or another generator-side metric you care about) from `grafanacloud-k6` for the Performance project you use in this lab.

1. Ensure a second panel or query shows quickpizza request volume or error rate from your Prometheus-compatible data source, using the same label filters you validated in the previous milestone.

1. Dock the Grafana tab beside your Grafana Cloud k6 tab, or arrange windows so you can see **Run** progress and both metrics at once. Set the time range to **Last 15 minutes** or similar so the chart updates quickly when the test starts; you tighten the absolute window after the run in later milestones.

1. Write one sentence in your notes describing what you expect to see when load ramps, so you can compare expectation to the live curves during the spike.

Check your work: you can move on when both signals render on screen without errors, your layout stays visible while you operate Grafana Cloud k6, and you captured the expectation note. If panels fail after refresh, reopen **Explore**, reselect data sources, then open the troubleshooting topics for this milestone.

In the next milestone you run the spike script, keep this view visible while the test executes, and record the UTC start time.
